(investigate), review (evaluate), coordinate (sync), admin (housekeeping), plan (strategize).\\nSpecializations: task.coordinate.meeting, task.coordinate.standup, task.execute.deploy, task.execute.checklist.\\nDefault: task.execute\"\n        },\n        \"state_key\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"default\": \"todo\",\n          \"description\": \"Initial state    }\n      },\n      \"required\": [\n        \"project_id\",\n        \"title\"\n      ]\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"update_onto_task\",\n      \"description\": \"Update an existing task in the ontology system.\\nCan modify title, description, state, priority, assignees, scheduling, and custom properties.\\nOnly updates fields that are provided - omitted fields remain unchanged.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"task_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Task UUID (required)\"\n          },\n          \"project_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Optional project UUID used for assignee handle resolution. If omitted, the task project is resolved automatically.\"\n          },\n          \"title\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"New task title\"\n          },\n          \"description\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"New description\"\n          },\n          \"update_strategy\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"enum\": [\n              \"replace\",\n              \"append\",\n              \"merge_llm\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"How to apply description updates: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing description.\"\n          },\n          \"merge_instructions\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Optional guidance when merging description text (e.g., keep bullets, integrate notes). Used with append/merge_llm.\"\n          },\n          \"type_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}]. Modes: execute, create, refine, research, review, coordinate, admin, plan.\"\n          },\n          \"state_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"New state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)\"\n          },\n          \"priority\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"description\": \"New priority (1-5)\"\n          },\n          \"assignee_actor_ids\": {\n            \"type\": \"array\",\n            \"description\": \"Optional full replacement assignee actor ID list (UUIDs) for active project members only. Prefer assignee_handles unless IDs were just retrieved from project members. Use [] to clear assignees. Max 10.\",\n            \"items\": {\n              \"type\": \"string\"\n            }\n          },\n          \"assignee_handles\": {\n            \"type\": \"array\",\n            \"description\": \"Optional assignee handles like [\\\"@jim\\\"]. Resolved against active project members by name/email local-part.\",\n            \"items\": {\n              \"type\": \"string\"\n            }\n          },\n          \"goal_id\": {\n            \"type\": [\n              \"string\",\n              \"null\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"Optional goal UUID that this task supports (null clears)\"\n          },\n          \"supporting_milestone_id\": {\n            \"type\": [\n              \"string\",\n              \"null\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"Optional milestone UUID this task targets (null clears)\"\n          },\n          \"start_at\": {\n            \"type\": [\n              \"string\",\n              \"null\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"New start date (ISO format) or null to clear\"\n          },\n          \"due_at\": {\n            \"type\": [\n              \"string\",\n              \"null\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"New due date (ISO format) or null to clear\"\n          },\n          \"props\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"description\": \"Properties to merge with existing props\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"task_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    \"name\": \"update_onto_task\",\n    \"description\": \"Update an existing task in the ontology system.\\nCan modify title, description, state, priority, assignees, scheduling, and custom properties.\\nOnly updates fields that are provided - omitted fields remain unchanged.\",\n    \"parameters\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"task_id\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Task UUID (required)\"\n        },\n        \"project_id\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional project UUID used for assignee handle resolution. If omitted, the task project is resolved automatically.\"\n        },\n        \"title\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"New task title\"\n        },\n        \"description\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"New description\"\n        },\n        \"update_strategy\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"enum\": [\n            \"replace\",\n            \"append\",\n            \"merge_llm\"\n          ],\n          \"description\": \"How to apply description updates: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing description.\"\n        },\n        \"merge_instructions\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional guidance when merging description text (e.g., keep bullets, integrate notes). Used with append/merge_llm.\"\n        },\n        \"type_key\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}]. Modes: execute, create, refine, research, review, coordinate, admin, plan.\"\n        },\n        \"state_key\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"New state (todo, in_progress, blocked, done)\"\n        },\n        \"priority\": {\n          \"type\": \"number\",\n          \"description\": \"New priority (1-5)\"\n        },\n        \"assignee_actor_ids\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional full replacement assignee actor ID list (UUIDs) for active project members only. Prefer assignee_handles unless IDs were just retrieved from project members. Use [] to clear assignees. Max 10.\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"assignee_handles\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional assignee handles like [\\\"@jim\\\"]. Resolved against active project members by name/email local-part.\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"goal_id\": {\n          \"type\": [\n            \"string\",\n            \"null\"\n          ],\n          \"description\": \"Optional goal UUID that this task supports (null clears)\"\n        },\n        \"supporting_milestone_id\": {\n          \"type\": [\n            \"string\",\n            \"null\"\n          ],\n          \"description\": \"Optional milestone UUID this task targets (null clears)\"\n        },\n        \"start_at\": {\n          \"type\": [\n            \"string\",\n            \"null\"\n          ],\n          \"description\": \"New start date (ISO format) or null to clear\"\n        },\n        \"due_at\": {\n          \"type\": [\n            \"string\",\n            \"null\"\n          ],\n          \"description\": \"New due date (ISO format) or null to clear\"\n        },\n        \"props\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"description\": \"Properties to merge with existing props\"\n        }\n      },\n      \"required\": [\n        \"task_id\"\n      ]\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"list_onto_documents\",\n      \"description\": \"List documents from the ontology system (onto_documents table). Returns document summaries plus markdown heading outlines (H1/H2/H3), not full body content.\\nUse get_onto_document_details when full document content is needed.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"project_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Filter documents by project ID\"\n          },\n          \"type_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Filter by document type key (e.g., document.context.project, document.knowledge.research)\"\n          },\n          \"state_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Filter by document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)\"\n          },\n          \"limit\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"default\": 20,\n            \"maximum\": 50,\n            \"description\": \"Maximum number of documents to return\"\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"get_onto_document_details\",\n      \"description\": \"Get complete details for a specific ontology document including content, description, and metadata.\\nUse when you need the full document before editing or linking it.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"document_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Document ID to retrieve\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"document_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"create_onto_document\",\n      \"description\": \"Create a new document in the ontology system.\\nUse for briefs, specs, context docs, or research artifacts linked to a project.\\nDocuments are organized in a hierarchical tree structure. Use parent_id to place the document under a parent folder.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"project_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Project UUID (required)\"\n          },\n          \"title\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Document title (required)\"\n          },\n          \"description\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Short summary of the document (required)\"\n          },\n          \"type_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"default\": \"document.default\",\n            \"description\": \"Document type taxonomy (required): document.{family}[.{variant}]\\nFamilies: context (project/brief), knowledge (research/brain_dump), notes (meeting_notes/rfc), spec (product/technical), reference (handbook/sop), intake (client/project).\\nExamples: document.context.project, document.knowledge.research, document.spec.technical\"\n          },\n          \"state_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)\",\n            \"default\": \"draft\"\n          },\n          \"content\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Markdown body content stored in the content column\"\n          },\n          \"props\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"description\": \"Additional properties/metadata\"\n          },\n          \"parent_id\": {\n            \"type\": [\n              \"string\",\n              \"null\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"Parent document ID to place this document under in the tree hierarchy. Null or omitted places at root level.\"\n          },\n          \"position\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"description\": \"Position among siblings (0-indexed). Omit to place at end.\"\n          },\n          \"parent\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"description\": \"Optional parent reference for semantic linking (legacy)\",\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"kind\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              },\n              \"id\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              },\n              \"is_primary\": {\n                \"type\": \"boolean\"\n              }\n            }\n          },\n          \"parents\": {\n            \"type\": \"array\",\n            \"description\": \"Optional multiple semantic parents (legacy)\",\n            \"items\": {\n              \"type\": \"object\",\n              \"properties\": {\n                \"kind\": {\n                  \"type\": \"string\"\n                },\n                \"id\": {\n                  \"type\": \"string\"\n                },\n                \"is_primary\": {\n                  \"type\": \"boolean\"\n                }\n              }\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"project_id\",\n          \"title\",\n          \"description\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    \"name\": \"create_onto_document\",\n    \"description\": \"Create a new document in the ontology system.\\nUse for briefs, specs, context docs, or research artifacts linked to a project.\\nDocuments are organized in a hierarchical tree structure. Use parent_id to place the document under a parent folder.\",\n    \"parameters\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"project_id\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Project UUID (required)\"\n        },\n        \"title\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Document title (required)\"\n        },\n        \"description\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Short summary of the document (required)\"\n        },\n        \"type_key\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"default\": \"document.default\",\n          \"description\": \"Document type taxonomy (required): document.{family}[.{variant}]\\nFamilies: context (project/brief), knowledge (research/brain_dump), notes (meeting_notes/rfc), spec (product/technical), reference (handbook/sop), intake (client/project).\\nExamples: document.context.project, document.knowledge.research, document.spec.technical\"\n        },\n        \"state_key\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)\",\n          \"default\": \"draft\"\n        },\n        \"content\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Markdown body content stored in the content column\"\n        },\n        \"props\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"description\": \"Additional properties/metadata\"\n        },\n        \"parent_id\": {\n          \"type\": [\n            \"string\",\n            \"null\"\n          ],\n          \"description\": \"Parent document ID to place this document under in the tree hierarchy. Null or omitted places at root level.\"\n        },\n        \"position\": {\n          \"type\": \"number\",\n          \"description\": \"Position among siblings (0-indexed). Omit to place at end.\"\n        },\n        \"parent\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional parent reference for semantic linking (legacy)\",\n          \"properties\": {\n            \"kind\": {\n              \"type\": \"string\"\n            },\n            \"id\": {\n              \"type\": \"string\"\n            },\n            \"is_primary\": {\n              \"type\": \"boolean\"\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        \"parents\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional multiple semantic parents (legacy)\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"kind\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              },\n              \"id\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              },\n              \"is_primary\": {\n                \"type\": \"boolean\"\n              }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"required\": [\n        \"project_id\",\n        \"title\",\n        \"description\"\n      ]\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"update_onto_document\",\n      \"description\": \"Update an existing ontology document.\\nUse for edits to titles, states, body markdown, or metadata.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"document_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Document UUID (required)\"\n          },\n          \"title\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"New document title\"\n          },\n          \"type_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"New document type key\"\n          },\n          \"state_key\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Document state (draft, in_review, ready, published, archived)\"\n          },\n          \"content\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Markdown content to store in the content column\"\n          },\n          \"description\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Short summary of the document\"\n          },\n          \"update_strategy\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"How to apply content: 'replace' (default), 'append', or 'merge_llm' to intelligently merge with existing content.\",\n            \"enum\": [\n              \"replace\",\n              \"append\",\n              \"merge_llm\"\n            ],\n            \"default\": \"replace\"\n          },\n          \"merge_instructions\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Optional guidance when merging content (e.g., keep headers, preserve tables, integrate research notes). Used with append/merge_llm.\"\n          },\n          \"props\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"description\": \"Metadata fields to merge into document props\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"document_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"get_document_tree\",\n      \"description\": \"Get the hierarchical document tree structure for a project.\\nReturns the tree structure; set include_documents to include document metadata and unlinked docs.\\nDocuments are organized in a wiki-like tree structure with folders (documents that have children) and leaf documents.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"project_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Project ID to get the document tree for (required)\"\n          },\n          \"include_documents\": {\n            \"type\": \"boolean\",\n            \"default\": false,\n            \"description\": \"Include document metadata and unlinked document list. Set true when you need titles or orphaned docs.\"\n          },\n          \"include_content\": {\n            \"type\": \"boolean\",\n            \"default\": false,\n            \"description\": \"Include full document content bodies. Only applies when include_documents is true.\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"project_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"move_document_in_tree\",\n      \"description\": \"Move or insert an existing document within the project's doc_structure.\\nUse this to nest existing or unlinked documents under a parent or reorder siblings.\\nIf new_parent_id is null or omitted, the document is placed at root level.\\nAlways pass the exact document_id from get_document_tree/list_onto_documents (do not pass document titles).\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"project_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Project UUID (required)\"\n          },\n          \"document_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Document UUID to move (required)\"\n          },\n          \"new_parent_id\": {\n            \"type\": [\n              \"string\",\n              \"null\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"New parent document ID (null or omitted places the document at root level).\"\n          },\n          \"new_position\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"default\": 0,\n            \"description\": \"Position among siblings (0-indexed). Omit to place at top.\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"project_id\",\n          \"document_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"get_project_calendar\",\n      \"description\": \"Get the project calendar mapping for a project.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"project_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Project id\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"project_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  {\n    \"type\": \"function\",\n    \"function\": {\n      \"name\": \"set_project_calendar\",\n      \"description\": \"Create or update a project calendar configuration.\",\n      \"parameters\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"project_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Project id\"\n          },\n          \"action\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"enum\": [\n              \"create\",\n              \"update\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"Force create or update (optional)\"\n          },\n          \"name\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Calendar name\"\n          },\n          \"description\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Calendar description\"\n          },\n          \"calendar_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Existing Google calendar id to link instead of creating a new calendar\"\n          },\n          \"color_id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Google color id for the calendar\"\n          },\n          \"sync_enabled\": {\n            \"type\": \"boolean\",\n            \"description\": \"Whether sync is enabled\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\n          \"project_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Execution Protocol\n\nThis section covers how to use tools safely. It combines the tool discovery workflow with the safety constraints for writes and ID handling.\n\n### Discovery workflow\n\n1. Start with current context, capabilities, and skill metadata to orient before searching.\n2. If the workflow is multi-step or easy to get wrong, load the relevant skill first.\n3. If a preloaded direct tool already fits the job, call it directly.\n4. Use `tool_search` only when the exact op is still unknown after context and skill guidance. Search for the operation you need, not workspace data. Good examples: `{\"capability\":\"overview\"}`, `{\"entity\":\"task\",\"kind\":\"write\",\"query\":\"update existing task state\"}`, and `{\"group\":\"onto\",\"entity\":\"document\",\"kind\":\"write\",\"query\":\"move document in tree\"}`.\n5. `tool_search` returns canonical ops plus direct tool names. If a search result is not already loaded, it becomes available as a direct tool for the next response in the same turn.\n6. Use `tool_schema` when an op is new in-turn or any write arguments are uncertain.\n7. After `tool_schema`, call the direct tool by name with concrete arguments. Do not route normal work through a generic executor.\n\n### Direct tool protocol\n\n1. The callable surface is the direct tool name, for example `update_onto_task({ task_id, state_key })` or `get_project_overview({ project_id })`.\n2. `tool_search` and `tool_schema` help you identify the exact tool and exact arguments, but they are not the final action for normal reads and writes.\n3. Put dynamic fields such as `task_id`, `project_id`, `title`, `state_key`, or `parent_id` directly in the tool arguments.\n4. If any required field is missing, do not execute. Ask one concise question or resolve it with a read op first.\n\n### Safe execution rules\n\n- Always pass valid, concrete tool arguments — never guess.\n- Reuse IDs and field values already present in structured context, recent history, or prior tool results. Avoid redundant reads.\n- **Never truncate, abbreviate, or elide IDs.** Pass the full exact UUID for every `*_id` or `entity_id` argument — no `\"...\"`, prefixes, or short forms.\n- **Never use placeholders.** Do not pass `\"__TASK_ID_FROM_ABOVE__\"`, `\"<task_id_uuid>\"`, `\"REPLACE_ME\"`, `\"TBD\"`, `\"none\"`, `\"null\"`, or `\"undefined\"` in any `*_id` field.\n- If a required ID is unknown, fetch it first with a read/list/search op — or ask one concise clarifying question. Do not emit a write that depends on a missing ID.\n- Before any update or delete, confirm the entity's exact UUID from current structured context and copy it directly into the args.\n- When multiple related changes are needed, batch them in a single turn rather than asking the user to confirm each one.\n- Do not use tools speculatively. If you do not yet know the schema or required fields, run `tool_schema` first.\n- `tool_search` is for discovering which op/tool to use. Query for operations like `\"update existing task state\"` or `\"move document in tree\"`, not workspace entities by name. Use ontology search/list/get ops to find actual projects, tasks, documents, goals, plans, milestones, and risks.\n- Only call `onto.<entity>.get`, `onto.<entity>.update`, or `onto.<entity>.delete` when you have the exact `*_id`.\n\n### Entity resolution order\n\n1. Reuse exact IDs already in structured context, recent history, or prior tool results when the user's follow-up clearly points to one of them.\n2. If the entity is not yet known, search within the current project first when project scope is known.\n3. If project scope is unknown or project search does not resolve the target, search across the workspace.\n4. If search returns multiple plausible matches, ask one concise clarification question before writing.\n\n## Agent Behavior\n\nThis section covers what to do and how to communicate while doing it.\n\n### Information capture\n\n- Capture **all** details the user provides: names, descriptions, dates, dependencies, context. Do not summarize away specifics.\n- Route information to the right entities immediately. If a task has a deadline, create the task and set the deadline in one pass. If a goal is mentioned while discussing a task, note the relationship.\n- For brain dumps, process everything — create multiple entities, link them, and update existing ones. Do not ask the user to repeat details you already have.\n- Prefer action over clarification. If you have enough to create something meaningful, do it. Refine later. Only ask when you truly cannot proceed.\n- Do not claim actions you did not perform.\n\n### Communication pattern\n\n**Always respond with text before making tool calls.** Users see your response as a live stream — going straight to tool calls leaves them waiting with nothing. Every turn should open with a brief message describing what you are about to do:\n\n- *\"Got it, let me create that task and link it to the milestone.\"*\n- *\"I'll update the goal description and check if there are related tasks that need adjusting.\"*\n- *\"Let me look at the current plan to see where this fits.\"*\n\nKeep the lead-in to 1-2 sentences, then make your tool calls. After tool calls complete, summarize what happened and surface any follow-ups.\n\nNever output scratchpad or self-correction text — no partial JSON, no internal notes, no visible \"No, fix args\".\n\n### Error handling\n\n- If data is missing or a tool fails, state what happened and request the minimum next input or retry.\n\n### Proactive intelligence\n\nAfter handling what was asked, think ahead:\n\n- What are the natural next steps for this work? Suggest them.\n- Are there related tasks, goals, or plans this affects? Check and flag connections.\n- Does this change the timeline or priority of anything else? Surface it.\n- Is anything missing from the project that should exist given what was just discussed (for example, a task with no parent plan, or a goal with no milestones)?\n- Are there risks or blockers the user might not be thinking about?\n\nKeep proactive suggestions brief and actionable — 1-2 sentences each, not essays.\n\n## Data Rules\n\n### Entity relationships\n\nIdeal structure builds over time — do not over-infer missing layers in early projects:\n\n- Projects should have goals.\n- Goals can have milestones.\n- Milestones can have plans.\n- Plans contain tasks.\n- Projects can also have events.\n\n### Document hierarchy\n\n- Documents have a hierarchical tree view defined by the `doc_structure` JSON reference.\n- Do not create edges between documents.\n- Do not use `onto.project.graph.reorganize` to reorganize documents.\n- Other entities may link to documents as references.\n- To nest or rehome existing docs (including unlinked docs), use `onto.document.tree.move` with the exact `document_id` and `new_position`. Use `new_parent_id` only when nesting under a parent — omit it for root moves.\n- To identify unlinked docs, call `onto.document.tree.get` with `include_documents=true`.\n- For \"link unlinked docs\" requests: call `onto.document.tree.get` once, then issue `onto.document.tree.move` for each unlinked document. Do not repeat `tree.get` unless a move fails and you need refreshed IDs.\n\n### Member roles\n\nWhen project context includes members:\n\n- Prefer assigning work to members whose `role_name` / `role_description` aligns with the responsibility.\n- Treat permission role and access as hard constraints — do not route admin actions to viewers.\n- If multiple members overlap responsibilities, ask one concise clarification before assigning ownership.\n</instructions>\n\n<context>\n<context_description>\nContext type: project. The assistant is working inside the project \"The Last Ember\" and should prioritize that project's entities, relationships, and next steps.\n</context_description>\n\n<project>\n<project_id>ba41311f-d777-46a3-a56b-3a99f84bc335</project_id>\n<project_name>The Last Ember</project_name>\n</project>\n\n{\n  \"goals\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"cf945f4b-ed8c-4e6d-be46-328c900b8ec7\",\n      \"name\": \"Write and complete the first fantasy novel 'The Last Ember'\",\n      \"state_key\": \"draft\",\n      \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-10T20:06:22.073177+00:00\",\n      \"description\": \"Main plot: A young blacksmith (orphan, raised by master blacksmith) discovers she can forge magical weapons when the kingdom's last dragon dies and darkness threatens the realm (kingdom of Aethermoor). Integrates magic system (emotions temper weapons) and world-building (Forge Temples, quenching ritual).\"\n          }\n        ],\n        \"description\": null\n      }\n    ],\n    \"version\": 6\n  },\n  \"events_window\": {\n    \"end_at\": \"2026-04-24T22:58:15.076Z\",\n    \"now_at\": \"2026-04-10T22:58:15.076Z\",\n    \"start_at\": \"2026-04-03T22:58:15.076Z\",\n    \"timezone\": \"UTC\",\n    \"past_days\": 7,\n    \"future_days\": 14\n  }\n}\n</context>\n\n[2] role=user\nSetting up my writing schedule for the next 3 months. Goal is to finish first draft by March 31st.\n\nDaily writing goal: 1,000 words minimum, Monday through Friday. Writing time: 5am-7am before work.\n\nWeekly tasks:\n\n- Saturday mornings: Chapter revision and editing\n- Sunday afternoons: Plot planning for next week\n\nMonthly milestones:\n\n- January: Complete chapters 1-10 (30,000 words)\n- February: Complete chapters 11-20 (30,000 words)\n- March: Complete chapters 21-30 and epilogue (35,000 words)\n\nAlso need to:\n\n- Join local writers' critique group (meets 1st Tuesday of month)\n- Submit chapter 1 to beta readers by January 15th\n- Research literary agents for fantasy genre\n"
```json
}
```

### 14. 2026-04-10T22:58:41.168668+00:00 · Turn Event: tool_result_received

- Event ID: turn_event:6ed16042-c14f-4a39-a5bf-e0554a35962e
- Type: turn_event
- Severity: info
- Turn: 1
- Summary: phase=tool • tool=skill_load

#### Payload

```json
{
	"id": "6ed16042-c14f-4a39-a5bf-e0554a35962e",
	"turn_run_id": "aacafff9-a2f5-4d7d-8f12-3fc461095be0",
	"stream_run_id": "285cb6bd-158f-4440-9274-a1873ac38018",
	"sequence_index": 13,
	"phase": "tool",
	"event_type": "tool_result_received",
	"tool_call_id": "call_80103040",
	"tool_name": "skill_load",
	"canonical_op": null,
	"success": true,
	"error": null,
	"duration_ms": null,
	"arguments": {
		"skill": "plan_management",
		"format": "full"
	},
	"result": {
		"id": "plan_management",
		"name": "Plan Management",
		"type": "skill",
		"notes": [
			"Plans are useful when work needs coordination or structure, not as a mandatory layer for every request.",
			"If the model already knows the exact task and plan relationships in-turn, it can go straight to `tool_schema` or the direct create/update tool."
		],
		"format": "full",
		"summary": "Plan workflow playbook for deciding when to create plans, structuring them well, and connecting plans to tasks, goals, milestones, and documents.",
		"version": "tool-registry/3f8e4f7d",
		"examples": [
			{
				"next_steps": [
					"Confirm the outcome the plan supports and whether a plan is actually warranted.",
					"If the create shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.create\" })`.",
					"Then call `create_onto_plan({ ... })`, followed by `create_onto_task({ ... })` for a small set of tasks with `plan_id`."
				],
				"description": "Turn a goal into an actionable first plan"
			},
			{
				"next_steps": [
					"Read the existing plan and current tasks first.",
					"If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.update\" })`.",
					"Then update the plan metadata with `update_onto_plan({ ... })` as needed.",
					"Add, adjust, or retire tasks so the plan matches the current approach."
				],
				"description": "Refine an existing plan that drifted out of date"
			}
		],
		"markdown": "# Plan Management\n\nPlan workflow playbook for deciding when to create plans, structuring them well, and connecting plans to tasks, goals, milestones, and documents.\n\n## When to Use\n- Decide whether a goal needs a plan\n- Create a plan from a goal or milestone\n- Break a plan into tasks\n- Refine a plan that already exists\n- Connect plans to supporting documents\n\n## Workflow\n1. Decide whether a plan is warranted. Prefer direct tasks when the work is trivial.\n2. Identify the outcome the plan supports.\n3. Create the plan with project_id and name; include description, type_key, and state_key whenever the user has given enough information.\n4. If goal or milestone IDs are already known, prefer passing them on plan creation; use onto.edge.link when adding relationships after the fact.\n5. Break the plan into concrete tasks.\n6. When creating tasks under a plan, include plan_id or the equivalent containment reference.\n7. Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done; do not invent values like open.\n8. Reference relevant documents when they materially shape execution.\n9. For plan updates, if the current structured context or prior turn already shows the exact plan_id, reuse it directly instead of searching again or emitting an empty update payload.\n\n## Related Tools\n- `onto.plan.create`\n- `onto.plan.get`\n- `onto.plan.update`\n- `onto.task.create`\n- `onto.task.list`\n- `onto.edge.link`\n- `onto.document.get`\n\n## Guardrails\n- Do not create a large plan when the request is still vague brainstorming.\n- Do not use the goal name as the plan name without adding an approach or phase.\n- Do not leave tasks floating if they are clearly part of a plan.\n\n## Examples\n### Turn a goal into an actionable first plan\n- Confirm the outcome the plan supports and whether a plan is actually warranted.\n- If the create shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.create\" })`.\n- Then call `create_onto_plan({ ... })`, followed by `create_onto_task({ ... })` for a small set of tasks with `plan_id`.\n### Refine an existing plan that drifted out of date\n- Read the existing plan and current tasks first.\n- If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.update\" })`.\n- Then update the plan metadata with `update_onto_plan({ ... })` as needed.\n- Add, adjust, or retire tasks so the plan matches the current approach.\n\n## Notes\n- Plans are useful when work needs coordination or structure, not as a mandatory layer for every request.\n- If the model already knows the exact task and plan relationships in-turn, it can go straight to `tool_schema` or the direct create/update tool.",
		"workflow": [
			"1) Decide whether a plan is warranted. Prefer direct tasks when the work is trivial.",
			"2) Identify the outcome the plan supports.",
			"3) Create the plan with project_id and name; include description, type_key, and state_key whenever the user has given enough information.",
			"4) If goal or milestone IDs are already known, prefer passing them on plan creation; use onto.edge.link when adding relationships after the fact.",
			"5) Break the plan into concrete tasks.",
			"6) When creating tasks under a plan, include plan_id or the equivalent containment reference.",
			"7) Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done; do not invent values like open.",
			"8) Reference relevant documents when they materially shape execution.",
			"9) For plan updates, if the current structured context or prior turn already shows the exact plan_id, reuse it directly instead of searching again or emitting an empty update payload."
		],
		"guardrails": [
			"Do not create a large plan when the request is still vague brainstorming.",
			"Do not use the goal name as the plan name without adding an approach or phase.",
			"Do not leave tasks floating if they are clearly part of a plan."
		],
		"description": "Plan workflow playbook for deciding when to create plans, structuring them well, and connecting plans to tasks, goals, milestones, and documents.",
		"related_ops": [

			"onto.plan.get",
			"onto.plan.update",
			"onto.task.create",
			"onto.task.list",
			"onto.edge.link",
			"onto.document.get"

				"7) Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done; do not invent values like open.",
				"8) Reference relevant documents when they materially shape execution.",
				"9) For plan updates, if the current structured context or prior turn already shows the exact plan_id, reuse it directly instead of searching again or emitting an empty update payload."
			],
			"guardrails": [
				"Do not create a large plan when the request is still vague brainstorming.",
				"Do not use the goal name as the plan name without adding an approach or phase.",
				"Do not leave tasks floating if they are clearly part of a plan."
			],
			"description": "Plan workflow playbook for deciding when to create plans, structuring them well, and connecting plans to tasks, goals, milestones, and documents.",

				"format": "full",
				"summary": "Plan workflow playbook for deciding when to create plans, structuring them well, and connecting plans to tasks, goals, milestones, and documents.",
				"version": "tool-registry/3f8e4f7d",
				"examples": [

							"If the create shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.create\" })`.",
							"Then call `create_onto_plan({ ... })`, followed by `create_onto_task({ ... })` for a small set of tasks with `plan_id`."

						"next_steps": [
							"Read the existing plan and current tasks first.",
							"If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.update\" })`.",
							"Then update the plan metadata with `update_onto_plan({ ... })` as needed.",
							"Add, adjust, or retire tasks so the plan matches the current approach."
						],
						"description": "Refine an existing plan that drifted out of date"
					}
				],
				"markdown": "# Plan Management\n\nPlan workflow playbook for deciding when to create plans, structuring them well, and connecting plans to tasks, goals, milestones, and documents.\n\n## When to Use\n- Decide whether a goal needs a plan\n- Create a plan from a goal or milestone\n- Break a plan into tasks\n- Refine a plan that already exists\n- Connect plans to supporting documents\n\n## Workflow\n1. Decide whether a plan is warranted. Prefer direct tasks when the work is trivial.\n2. Identify the outcome the plan supports.\n3. Create the plan with project_id and name; include description, type_key, and state_key whenever the user has given enough information.\n4. If goal or milestone IDs are already known, prefer passing them on plan creation; use onto.edge.link when adding relationships after the fact.\n5. Break the plan into concrete tasks.\n6. When creating tasks under a plan, include plan_id or the equivalent containment reference.\n7. Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done; do not invent values like open.\n8. Reference relevant documents when they materially shape execution.\n9. For plan updates, if the current structured context or prior turn already shows the exact plan_id, reuse it directly instead of searching again or emitting an empty update payload.\n\n## Related Tools\n- `onto.plan.create`\n- `onto.plan.get`\n- `onto.plan.update`\n- `onto.task.create`\n- `onto.task.list`\n- `onto.edge.link`\n- `onto.document.get`\n\n## Guardrails\n- Do not create a large plan when the request is still vague brainstorming.\n- Do not use the goal name as the plan name without adding an approach or phase.\n- Do not leave tasks floating if they are clearly part of a plan.\n\n## Examples\n### Turn a goal into an actionable first plan\n- Confirm the outcome the plan supports and whether a plan is actually warranted.\n- If the create shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.create\" })`.\n- Then call `create_onto_plan({ ... })`, followed by `create_onto_task({ ... })` for a small set of tasks with `plan_id`.\n### Refine an existing plan that drifted out of date\n- Read the existing plan and current tasks first.\n- If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.plan.update\" })`.\n- Then update the plan metadata with `update_onto_plan({ ... })` as needed.\n- Add, adjust, or retire tasks so the plan matches the current approach.\n\n## Notes\n- Plans are useful when work needs coordination or structure, not as a mandatory layer for every request.\n- If the model already knows the exact task and plan relationships in-turn, it can go straight to `tool_schema` or the direct create/update tool.",
				"workflow": [
					"1) Decide whether a plan is warranted. Prefer direct tasks when the work is trivial.",
					"2) Identify the outcome the plan supports.",
					"3) Create the plan with project_id and name; include description, type_key, and state_key whenever the user has given enough information.",
					"4) If goal or milestone IDs are already known, prefer passing them on plan creation; use onto.edge.link when adding relationships after the fact.",
					"5) Break the plan into concrete tasks.",
					"6) When creating tasks under a plan, include plan_id or the equivalent containment reference.",
					"7) Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done; do not invent values like open.",
					"8) Reference relevant documents when they materially shape execution.",
					"9) For plan updates, if the current structured context or prior turn already shows the exact plan_id, reuse it directly instead of searching again or emitting an empty update payload."
				],
				"guardrails": [
					"Do not create a large plan when the request is still vague brainstorming.",
					"Do not use the goal name as the plan name without adding an approach or phase.",
					"Do not leave tasks floating if they are clearly part of a plan."
				],

		"notes": [
```
```json
			"Calendar reads and writes are often sensitive to scope, time zone normalization, and exact event identifiers.",
			"Use `tool_schema` if the request depends on less common fields such as sync_to_calendar or calendar_id."

		"format": "full",
		"summary": "Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.",
		"version": "tool-registry/3f8e4f7d",

				"next_steps": [

					"Then call `create_calendar_event({ ... })` with title, start_at, project_id, `calendar_scope: \"project\"`, and task_id when relevant."

				"description": "Schedule a project work session tied to a task"
			},
			{
				"next_steps": [
					"Use cal.event.list or cal.event.get to discover the exact onto_event_id or event_id.",
					"If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"cal.event.update\" })`.",

				"description": "Reschedule an existing event safely"

		],
		"markdown": "# Calendar Operations\n\nCalendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n\n## When to Use\n- Read events in a time window\n- Create, reschedule, or cancel events\n- Choose between user scope and project scope\n- Manage project calendar mapping\n- Link work sessions to tasks\n\n## Workflow\n1. Choose scope first: user, project, or explicit calendar_id.\n2. For project scope, include exact project_id.\n3. Use timezone-safe ISO 8601 values for start_at and end_at, or supply timezone.\n4. For project calendar mapping questions, check cal.project.get before assuming a project calendar exists.\n5. For update/delete, discover and pass exact onto_event_id or event_id.\n6. For first-time or complex writes, inspect the exact tool schema with `tool_schema` before calling the direct calendar tool.\n7. After execution, tell the user what changed and mention sync implications when they matter.\n\n## Related Tools\n- `cal.event.list`\n- `cal.event.get`\n- `cal.event.create`\n- `cal.event.update`\n- `cal.event.delete`\n- `cal.project.get`\n- `cal.project.set`\n\n## Guardrails\n- Prefer onto_event_id when available for update/delete.\n- If sync status matters, verify with calendar ops instead of guessing.\n- If a task is clearly the subject of the event, include task_id.\n- If only start_at is known, the backend may default duration; still prefer explicit end_at when the user gave enough detail.\n\n## Examples\n### Schedule a project work session tied to a task\n- If the exact args are unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"cal.event.create\" })`.\n- Then call `create_calendar_event({ ... })` with title, start_at, project_id, `calendar_scope: \"project\"`, and task_id when relevant.\n### Reschedule an existing event safely\n- Use cal.event.list or cal.event.get to discover the exact onto_event_id or event_id.\n- If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"cal.event.update\" })`.\n- Then call `update_calendar_event({ ... })` with the exact identifier and updated fields.\n\n## Notes\n- Calendar reads and writes are often sensitive to scope, time zone normalization, and exact event identifiers.\n- Use `tool_schema` if the request depends on less common fields such as sync_to_calendar or calendar_id.",

After execution, tell the user what changed and mention sync implications when they matter.\n\n## Related Tools\n- `cal.event.list`\n- `cal.event.get`\n- `cal.event.create`\n- `cal.event.update`\n- `cal.event.delete`\n- `cal.project.get`\n- `cal.project.set`\n\n## Guardrails\n- Prefer onto_event_id when available for update/delete.\n- If sync status matters, verify with calendar ops instead of guessing.\n- If a task is clearly the subject of the event, include task_id.\n- If only start_at is known, the backend may default duration; still prefer explicit end_at when the user gave enough detail.\n\n## Examples\n### Schedule a project work session tied to a task\n- If the exact args are unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"cal.event.create\" })`.\n- Then call `create_calendar_event({ ... })` with title, start_at, project_id, `calendar_scope: \"project\"`, and task_id when relevant.\n### Reschedule an existing event safely\n- Use cal.event.list or cal.event.get to discover the exact onto_event_id or event_id.\n- If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"cal.event.update\" })`.\n- Then call `update_calendar_event({ ... })` with the exact identifier and updated fields.\n\n## Notes\n- Calendar reads and writes are often sensitive to scope, time zone normalization, and exact event identifiers.\n- Use `tool_schema` if the request depends on less common fields such as sync_to_calendar or calendar_id.",
			"workflow": [
				"1) Choose scope first: user, project, or explicit calendar_id.",
				"2) For project scope, include exact project_id.",
				"3) Use timezone-safe ISO 8601 values for start_at and end_at, or supply timezone.",
				"4) For project calendar mapping questions, check cal.project.get before assuming a project calendar exists.",
				"5) For update/delete, discover and pass exact onto_event_id or event_id.",
				"6) For first-time or complex writes, inspect the exact tool schema with `tool_schema` before calling the direct calendar tool.",
				"7) After execution, tell the user what changed and mention sync implications when they matter."
			],
			"guardrails": [
				"Prefer onto_event_id when available for update/delete.",
				"If sync status matters, verify with calendar ops instead of guessing.",
				"If a task is clearly the subject of the event, include task_id.",
				"If only start_at is known, the backend may default duration; still prefer explicit end_at when the user gave enough detail."
			],
			"description": "Calendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.",

				"markdown": "# Calendar Operations\n\nCalendar workflow playbook for BuildOS agentic chat. Use for event reads/writes, scope decisions, and project calendar mapping.\n\n## When to Use\n- Read events in a time window\n- Create, reschedule, or cancel events\n- Choose between user scope and project scope\n- Manage project calendar mapping\n- Link work sessions to tasks\n\n## Workflow\n1. Choose scope first: user, project, or explicit calendar_id.\n2. For project scope, include exact project_id.\n3. Use timezone-safe ISO 8601 values for start_at and end_at, or supply timezone.\n4. For project calendar mapping questions, check cal.project.get before assuming a project calendar exists.\n5. For update/delete, discover and pass exact onto_event_id or event_id.\n6. For first-time or complex writes, inspect the exact tool schema with `tool_schema` before calling the direct calendar tool.\n7. After execution, tell the user what changed and mention sync implications when they matter.\n\n## Related Tools\n- `cal.event.list`\n- `cal.event.get`\n- `cal.event.create`\n- `cal.event.update`\n- `cal.event.delete`\n- `cal.project.get`\n- `cal.project.set`\n\n## Guardrails\n- Prefer onto_event_id when available for update/delete.\n- If sync status matters, verify with calendar ops instead of guessing.\n- If a task is clearly the subject of the event, include task_id.\n- If only start_at is known, the backend may default duration; still prefer explicit end_at when the user gave enough detail.\n\n## Examples\n### Schedule a project work session tied to a task\n- If the exact args are unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"cal.event.create\" })`.\n- Then call `create_calendar_event({ ... })` with title, start_at, project_id, `calendar_scope: \"project\"`, and task_id when relevant.\n### Reschedule an existing event safely\n- Use cal.event.list or cal.event.get to discover the exact onto_event_id or event_id.\n- If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"cal.event.update\" })`.\n- Then call `update_calendar_event({ ... })` with the exact identifier and updated fields.\n\n## Notes\n- Calendar reads and writes are often sensitive to scope, time zone normalization, and exact event identifiers.\n- Use `tool_schema` if the request depends on less common fields such as sync_to_calendar or calendar_id.",
				"workflow": [

	"result": {
```
```json
		"id": "task_management",
		"name": "Task Management",
		"type": "skill",
		"notes": [
			"Tasks are for future human work, not a transcript of what happened in chat.",
			"Containment and task relationships matter. A well-placed task is usually better than a floating task with no parent context."
		],
		"format": "full",
		"summary": "Task workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.",
		"version": "tool-registry/3f8e4f7d",
		"examples": [
			{
				"next_steps": [
					"Confirm the request is future user work rather than work the agent can complete now.",
					"If the create shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.task.create\" })`.",
					"Then call `create_onto_task({ ... })` with the right parent plan/goal/milestone when that relationship is already clear.",
					"Example payload when project context is already known: `create_onto_task({ project_id: \"4cfdbed1-840a-4fe4-9751-77c7884daa70\", title: \"Revise chapter 2 dialogue between Elena and Master Thorne\", description: \"Strengthen the dialogue beats in the Elena and Master Thorne scenes from chapter 2.\", type_key: \"task.refine\" })`"
				],
				"description": "Track a real follow-up the user must do later"
			},
			{
				"next_steps": [
					"If the user names the next chapter or work item, turn that into the title instead of emitting a blank create call.",
					"Example: `create_onto_task({ project_id: \"4cfdbed1-840a-4fe4-9751-77c7884daa70\", title: \"Draft chapter 3: Elena's first magical forging attempt\", description: \"Include Elena's first forging attempt, introduce the Shadow King's herald, and foreshadow the prophecy.\", type_key: \"task.create\" })`"
				],
				"description": "Create the next drafting task from a progress update"
			},
			{
				"next_steps": [
					"If the prior assistant turn or structured context already surfaced the exact task_id, reuse it directly.",
					"Otherwise use onto.task.search, onto.task.list, or onto.task.get to discover the exact task_id first.",
					"When project scope is known, prefer project-scoped task lookup before broader workspace lookup.",
					"If ownership is changing, prefer assignee_handles unless actor IDs were just retrieved.",
					"If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.task.update\" })`.",
					"Then call `update_onto_task({ ... })` with the exact task_id and the intended state/assignment changes.",
					"Example when the task is already in structured context: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", state_key: \"done\" })`",
					"Example when the task needs a richer update: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", description: \"Chapter 1 and chapter 2 are complete. Update the outline to reflect the revised chapter 3 beats and continuity fixes.\", update_strategy: \"append\" })`"
				],
				"description": "Update an existing task status and owner safely"
			},
			{
				"next_steps": [
					"Never emit `update_onto_task({})`.",
					"Never emit `create_onto_task({ project_id })` without a concrete `title`.",
					"If you know the task from context, copy its exact `task_id` into the update call.",
					"If you do not know the exact `task_id`, resolve it first or ask one concise clarifying question."
				],
				"description": "Guard against empty task writes"
			}
		],
		"markdown": "# Task Management\n\nTask workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.\n\n## When to Use\n- Decide whether a user request should become a task at all\n- Create a task for future human work\n- Assign or reassign task ownership\n- Update task state, dates, or priority\n- Place a task under the right plan, goal, or milestone\n\n## Workflow\n1. Decide first whether this should be a tracked task or whether the work should just be done in the conversation now.\n2. If it should be a task, choose the project and the right parent context: plan, goal, milestone, or direct project scope.\n3. For creates, include title and only add schedule, assignees, or supporting links when the user has given enough concrete information.\n4. Prefer assignee_handles over actor IDs unless the IDs were just discovered in-turn from project membership data.\n5. Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done.\n6. For updates, reuse the exact task_id from recent context or the prior assistant turn when the follow-up clearly refers to that task. If the exact task_id is not already known, discover it before any write.\n7. Only use description merge strategies when append or merge behavior is actually needed; otherwise keep updates simple.\n8. After execution, tell the user what changed and call out any missing owner, due date, or parent relationship that still matters.\n\n## Related Tools\n- `onto.task.create`\n- `onto.task.get`\n- `onto.task.list`\n- `onto.task.search`\n- `onto.task.update`\n- `onto.plan.get`\n- `onto.goal.get`\n- `onto.milestone.get`\n\n## Guardrails\n- Do not create tasks for research, analysis, brainstorming, or drafting that the agent can do now in chat.\n- Do not invent assignee IDs, handles, or project membership.\n- Do not use invalid task states such as open.\n- Do not emit update calls without an exact task_id.\n- If the request is really a goal, milestone, or plan, do not flatten it into a task just because task creation is easy.\n\n## Examples\n### Track a real follow-up the user must do later\n- Confirm the request is future user work rather than work the agent can complete now.\n- If the create shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.task.create\" })`.\n- Then call `create_onto_task({ ... })` with the right parent plan/goal/milestone when that relationship is already clear.\n- Example payload when project context is already known: `create_onto_task({ project_id: \"4cfdbed1-840a-4fe4-9751-77c7884daa70\", title: \"Revise chapter 2 dialogue between Elena and Master Thorne\", description: \"Strengthen the dialogue beats in the Elena and Master Thorne scenes from chapter 2.\", type_key: \"task.refine\" })`\n### Create the next drafting task from a progress update\n- If the user names the next chapter or work item, turn that into the title instead of emitting a blank create call.\n- Example: `create_onto_task({ project_id: \"4cfdbed1-840a-4fe4-9751-77c7884daa70\", title: \"Draft chapter 3: Elena's first magical forging attempt\", description: \"Include Elena's first forging attempt, introduce the Shadow King's herald, and foreshadow the prophecy.\", type_key: \"task.create\" })`\n### Update an existing task status and owner safely\n- If the prior assistant turn or structured context already surfaced the exact task_id, reuse it directly.\n- Otherwise use onto.task.search, onto.task.list, or onto.task.get to discover the exact task_id first.\n- When project scope is known, prefer project-scoped task lookup before broader workspace lookup.\n- If ownership is changing, prefer assignee_handles unless actor IDs were just retrieved.\n- If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.task.update\" })`.\n- Then call `update_onto_task({ ... })` with the exact task_id and the intended state/assignment changes.\n- Example when the task is already in structured context: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", state_key: \"done\" })`\n- Example when the task needs a richer update: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", description: \"Chapter 1 and chapter 2 are complete. Update the outline to reflect the revised chapter 3 beats and continuity fixes.\", update_strategy: \"append\" })`\n### Guard against empty task writes\n- Never emit `update_onto_task({})`.\n- Never emit `create_onto_task({ project_id })` without a concrete `title`.\n- If you know the task from context, copy its exact `task_id` into the update call.\n- If you do not know the exact `task_id`, resolve it first or ask one concise clarifying question.\n\n## Notes\n- Tasks are for future human work, not a transcript of what happened in chat.\n- Containment and task relationships matter. A well-placed task is usually better than a floating task with no parent context.",
		"workflow": [
			"1) Decide first whether this should be a tracked task or whether the work should just be done in the conversation now.",
			"2) If it should be a task, choose the project and the right parent context: plan, goal, milestone, or direct project scope.",
			"3) For creates, include title and only add schedule, assignees, or supporting links when the user has given enough concrete information.",
			"4) Prefer assignee_handles over actor IDs unless the IDs were just discovered in-turn from project membership data.",
			"5) Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done.",
			"6) For updates, reuse the exact task_id from recent context or the prior assistant turn when the follow-up clearly refers to that task. If the exact task_id is not already known, discover it before any write.",
			"7) Only use description merge strategies when append or merge behavior is actually needed; otherwise keep updates simple.",
			"8) After execution, tell the user what changed and call out any missing owner, due date, or parent relationship that still matters."
		],
		"guardrails": [

						"Otherwise use onto.task.search, onto.task.list, or onto.task.get to discover the exact task_id first.",
						"When project scope is known, prefer project-scoped task lookup before broader workspace lookup.",
						"If ownership is changing, prefer assignee_handles unless actor IDs were just retrieved.",
						"If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.task.update\" })`.",
						"Then call `update_onto_task({ ... })` with the exact task_id and the intended state/assignment changes.",
						"Example when the task is already in structured context: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", state_key: \"done\" })`",
						"Example when the task needs a richer update: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", description: \"Chapter 1 and chapter 2 are complete. Update the outline to reflect the revised chapter 3 beats and continuity fixes.\", update_strategy: \"append\" })`"
					],
					"description": "Update an existing task status and owner safely"
				},
				{
					"next_steps": [
						"Never emit `update_onto_task({})`.",
						"Never emit `create_onto_task({ project_id })` without a concrete `title`.",
						"If you know the task from context, copy its exact `task_id` into the update call.",
						"If you do not know the exact `task_id`, resolve it first or ask one concise clarifying question."
					],
					"description": "Guard against empty task writes"
				}
			],
			"markdown": "# Task Management\n\nTask workflow playbook for deciding when work should become a task and how to manage task scope, ownership, schedule, and relationships safely.\n\n## When to Use\n- Decide whether a user request should become a task at all\n- Create a task for future human work\n- Assign or reassign task ownership\n- Update task state, dates, or priority\n- Place a task under the right plan, goal, or milestone\n\n## Workflow\n1. Decide first whether this should be a tracked task or whether the work should just be done in the conversation now.\n2. If it should be a task, choose the project and the right parent context: plan, goal, milestone, or direct project scope.\n3. For creates, include title and only add schedule, assignees, or supporting links when the user has given enough concrete information.\n4. Prefer assignee_handles over actor IDs unless the IDs were just discovered in-turn from project membership data.\n5. Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done.\n6. For updates, reuse the exact task_id from recent context or the prior assistant turn when the follow-up clearly refers to that task. If the exact task_id is not already known, discover it before any write.\n7. Only use description merge strategies when append or merge behavior is actually needed; otherwise keep updates simple.\n8. After execution, tell the user what changed and call out any missing owner, due date, or parent relationship that still matters.\n\n## Related Tools\n- `onto.task.create`\n- `onto.task.get`\n- `onto.task.list`\n- `onto.task.search`\n- `onto.task.update`\n- `onto.plan.get`\n- `onto.goal.get`\n- `onto.milestone.get`\n\n## Guardrails\n- Do not create tasks for research, analysis, brainstorming, or drafting that the agent can do now in chat.\n- Do not invent assignee IDs, handles, or project membership.\n- Do not use invalid task states such as open.\n- Do not emit update calls without an exact task_id.\n- If the request is really a goal, milestone, or plan, do not flatten it into a task just because task creation is easy.\n\n## Examples\n### Track a real follow-up the user must do later\n- Confirm the request is future user work rather than work the agent can complete now.\n- If the create shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.task.create\" })`.\n- Then call `create_onto_task({ ... })` with the right parent plan/goal/milestone when that relationship is already clear.\n- Example payload when project context is already known: `create_onto_task({ project_id: \"4cfdbed1-840a-4fe4-9751-77c7884daa70\", title: \"Revise chapter 2 dialogue between Elena and Master Thorne\", description: \"Strengthen the dialogue beats in the Elena and Master Thorne scenes from chapter 2.\", type_key: \"task.refine\" })`\n### Create the next drafting task from a progress update\n- If the user names the next chapter or work item, turn that into the title instead of emitting a blank create call.\n- Example: `create_onto_task({ project_id: \"4cfdbed1-840a-4fe4-9751-77c7884daa70\", title: \"Draft chapter 3: Elena's first magical forging attempt\", description: \"Include Elena's first forging attempt, introduce the Shadow King's herald, and foreshadow the prophecy.\", type_key: \"task.create\" })`\n### Update an existing task status and owner safely\n- If the prior assistant turn or structured context already surfaced the exact task_id, reuse it directly.\n- Otherwise use onto.task.search, onto.task.list, or onto.task.get to discover the exact task_id first.\n- When project scope is known, prefer project-scoped task lookup before broader workspace lookup.\n- If ownership is changing, prefer assignee_handles unless actor IDs were just retrieved.\n- If the update shape is unclear, call `tool_schema({ op: \"onto.task.update\" })`.\n- Then call `update_onto_task({ ... })` with the exact task_id and the intended state/assignment changes.\n- Example when the task is already in structured context: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", state_key: \"done\" })`\n- Example when the task needs a richer update: `update_onto_task({ task_id: \"440c2639-9000-4111-aeea-ee374f8fb925\", description: \"Chapter 1 and chapter 2 are complete. Update the outline to reflect the revised chapter 3 beats and continuity fixes.\", update_strategy: \"append\" })`\n### Guard against empty task writes\n- Never emit `update_onto_task({})`.\n- Never emit `create_onto_task({ project_id })` without a concrete `title`.\n- If you know the task from context, copy its exact `task_id` into the update call.\n- If you do not know the exact `task_id`, resolve it first or ask one concise clarifying question.\n\n## Notes\n- Tasks are for future human work, not a transcript of what happened in chat.\n- Containment and task relationships matter. A well-placed task is usually better than a floating task with no parent context.",
			"workflow": [
				"1) Decide first whether this should be a tracked task or whether the work should just be done in the conversation now.",
				"2) If it should be a task, choose the project and the right parent context: plan, goal, milestone, or direct project scope.",

				"4) Prefer assignee_handles over actor IDs unless the IDs were just discovered in-turn from project membership data.",
				"5) Use valid task states such as todo, in_progress, blocked, or done.",
				"6) For updates, reuse the exact task_id from recent context or the prior assistant turn when the follow-up clearly refers to that task. If the exact task_id is not already known, discover it before any write.",
				"7) Only use description merge strategies when append or merge behavior is actually needed; otherwise keep updates simple.",
				"8) After execution, tell the user what changed and call out any missing owner, due date, or parent relationship that still matters."
			],
```

```json
		"args": [
			{
```
```json
				"name": "project_id",
				"type": "string",
				"required": true,
				"description": "Project UUID (required) - the project this task belongs to"
			},
			{
				"name": "title",
				"type": "string",
				"required": true,
				"description": "Task title (required)"
			},
			{
				"name": "description",
				"type": "string",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Task description (optional)"
			},
			{
				"name": "type_key",
				"type": "string",
				"default": "task.execute",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}].\nModes: execute (action), create (produce), refine (improve), research (investigate), review (evaluate), coordinate (sync), admin (housekeeping), plan (strategize).\nSpecializations: task.coordinate.meeting, task.coordinate.standup, task.execute.deploy, task.execute.checklist.\nDefault: task.execute"
			},
			{
				"name": "state_key",
				"type": "string",
				"default": "todo",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Initial state (default: todo). Valid: todo, in_progress, blocked, done"
			},
			{
				"name": "priority",
				"type": "number",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Priority level (1-5, default: 3). Higher = more important"
			},
			{
				"name": "assignee_actor_ids",
				"type": "array",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Optional assignee actor IDs (UUIDs) for active project members only. Prefer assignee_handles unless IDs were just retrieved from project members. Replaces assignment list on create. Max 10."
			},
			{
				"name": "assignee_handles",
				"type": "array",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Optional assignee handles like [\"@jim\", \"@dj\"]. Handles resolve against active project members by name/email local-part."
			},
			{
				"name": "plan_id",
				"type": "string",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Optional plan UUID to associate this task with"
			},
			{
				"name": "goal_id",
				"type": "string",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Optional goal UUID that this task supports"
			},

				"type": "string",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Optional milestone UUID this task targets"
			},

				"name": "parent",
				"type": "object",
				"required": false,

			},
			{
				"name": "parents",
				"type": "array",
				"required": false,
				"description": "Optional multiple containment parents"
			},

				"description": "Additional properties as JSON object"

		"usage": "create_onto_task({ ... })",

				"Create tasks for human actions that must happen outside this chat"
			],
			"dont": [

					"type": "string",

					"description": "Work mode taxonomy: task.{work_mode}[.{specialization}].\nModes: execute (action), create (produce), refine (improve), research (investigate), review (evaluate), coordinate (sync), admin (housekeeping), plan (strategize).\nSpecializations: task.coordinate.meeting, task.coordinate.standup, task.execute.deploy, task.execute.checklist.\nDefault: task.execute"
				},
				"state_key": {
					"type": "string",
					"default": "todo",
					"description": "Initial state (default: todo). Valid: todo, in_progress, blocked, done"
				},
				"project_id": {

				},

							"id": {
								"type": "string"
							},

								"type": "string"
							},

								"type": "string"
							},

								"enum": ["containment", "semantic"],
								"type": "string"
							}
						}
					},

					},

		"id_args": ["project_id", "plan_id", "goal_id", "supporting_milestone_id"],
		"summary": "Create a new task in the ontology system.",
		"examples": [
			{
				"tool_call": {
					"name": "create_onto_task",
					"arguments": {
						"title": "<title>",
						"project_id": "<project_id_uuid>"
					}
				},
				"description": "Minimal valid call"
			}
		],
		"tool_name": "create_onto_task",
		"description": "Create a new task in the ontology system.\nCreates a task within a project and optionally assigns it to a plan and collaborators.\nAutomatically creates containment edges based on parent inputs (plan/goal/project).\n\n**CRITICAL: When to create tasks vs. when NOT to:**\n\nCREATE a task when:\n- The USER explicitly asks to \"add a task\", \"create a task\", \"track this\", or \"remind me to\"\n- The work requires HUMAN action (phone calls, meetings, reviews, approvals)\n- The work must happen OUTSIDE this conversation (external results, future actions)\n- The user is building a project plan and wants persistent task tracking\n\nDO NOT create a task when:\n- You (the agent) can help with the work RIGHT NOW in this conversation\n- The request is for research, analysis, brainstorming, or summarizing (just do it)\n- You're about to perform the action yourself (don't create then immediately complete)\n- You want to appear helpful or structured (only create if the user needs to track it)\n- The user is exploring ideas or asking questions (just respond helpfully)\n\nExamples:\n- \"Add a task to call the client tomorrow\" → CREATE (user must do this later)\n- \"Help me brainstorm marketing ideas\" → DO NOT CREATE (help them now)\n- \"I need to review the design mockups\" → CREATE (user action required)\n- \"What are my project blockers?\" → DO NOT CREATE (just analyze and respond)\n- \"Create tasks for the launch checklist\" → CREATE (explicit request)\n- \"Let's outline the API endpoints\" → DO NOT CREATE (collaborative work you can help with)\n- \"Remind me to follow up with Sarah\" → CREATE (future user action)\n\nRemember: Tasks should represent FUTURE USER WORK, not a log of what you discussed or helped with.",
		"callable_tool": "create_onto_task",
		"required_args": ["project_id", "title"],
		"example_tool_call": {
			"tool_call": {
				"name": "create_onto_task",
				"arguments": {
					"title": "<title>",
					"project_id": "<project_id_uuid>"
				}
			}
		}
	},

			"description": "Create a new task in the ontology system.\nCreates a task within a project and optionally assigns it to a plan and collaborators.\nAutomatically creates containment edges based on parent inputs (plan/goal/project).\n\n**CRITICAL: When to create tasks vs. when NOT to:**\n\nCREATE a task when:\n- The USER explicitly asks to \"add a task\", \"create a task\", \"track this\", or \"remind me to\"\n- The work requires HUMAN action (phone calls, meetings, reviews, approvals)\n- The work must happen OUTSIDE this conversation (external results, future actions)\n- The user is building a project plan and wants persistent task tracking\n\nDO NOT create a task when:\n- You (the agent) can help with the work RIGHT NOW in this conversation\n- The request is for research, analysis, brainstorming, or summarizing (just do it)\n- You're about to perform the action yourself (don't create then immediately complete)\n- You want to appear helpful or structured (only create if the user needs to track it)\n- The user is exploring ideas or asking questions (just respond helpfully)\n\nExamples:\n- \"Add a task to call the client tomorrow\" → CREATE (user must do this later)\n- \"Help me brainstorm marketing ideas\" → DO NOT CREATE (help them now)\n- \"I need to review the design mockups\" → CREATE (user action required)\n- \"What are my project blockers?\" → DO NOT CREATE (just analyze and respond)\n- \"Create tasks for the launch checklist\" → CREATE (explicit request)\n- \"Let's outline the API endpoints\" → DO NOT CREATE (collaborative work you can help with)\n- \"Remind me to follow up with Sarah\" → CREATE (future user action)\n\nRemember: Tasks should represent FUTURE USER WORK, not a log of what you discussed or helped with.",
```
